name: IRC_INTERACTIONS
description: This table stores the interaction for a person in the context of a Submission, Candidate Pool and Candidate General Profile.

columns.Name: INTERACTION_ID
columns.Comments: Primary key of the table. System generated.

columns.Name: PERSON_ID
columns.Comments: Stores ID of the person for whom the Interaction note is added

columns.Name: ADDED_BY_PERSON_ID
columns.Comments: Stores ID of the person creating the interaction

columns.Name: CONTEXT_TYPE_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores the context. Submission, Pool, General Profile etc. Lookup type is ORA_IRC_INTERACTION_CTXT_TYPE

columns.Name: CONTEXT_ID
columns.Comments: Stores the ID of the context object ??? Reference to SUBMISSION_ID or POOL_ID or PERSON_ID(In case of General Profile) etc

columns.Name: SUB_CONTEXT_ID
columns.Comments: added for tracking LinkedIn InMail session.  Every session will have the same SubContextId so they could be displayed together as interactions.

columns.Name: INTERACTION_TYPE_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores the interaction type. Email, Phone, In Person etc. This is a lookup. Lookup type ORA_IRC_INTERACTION_TYPE

columns.Name: INTERACTION_DATE
columns.Comments: Stores the date when the interaction happened.

columns.Name: TEXT
columns.Comments: Stores the text for this interaction.

columns.Name: MASKED_BODY_TEXT
columns.Comments: Store the body text without PII data

columns.Name: HIRING_TEAM_INTR_FLAG
columns.Comments: Flag to indicate whether the current interaction entry was sent to hiring team

columns.Name: SUBJECT
columns.Comments: Stores the subject for this interaction.

columns.Name: MESSAGE_IDENTIFIER
columns.Comments: Contains the message identifier for both email and SMS. For email stores the UMS SMTP message id and for SMS stores the id sent by the provider.

columns.Name: MESSAGE_STATUS
columns.Comments: Stores the status of the message

columns.Name: CONTENT_LIBRARY_ITEM_CODE
columns.Comments: The Recruiting Content Library's DESCRIPTION_CODE that points to the table IRC_DESCRIPTIONS_B.

columns.Name: RESEND_ALLOWED_STATUS
columns.Comments: Status controls the resend ability of the interaction

columns.Name: CONTENT_LIBRARY_ITEM_ID
columns.Comments: Contains the content library item identifier associated to the interaction. Foreign key to the table irc_descriptions_b

columns.Name: CHANNEL_TEMPLATE_ID
columns.Comments: Contains the channel specific template identifier associated to the interaction

columns.Name: INTERACTION_SUB_TYPE_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores additional details about the interaction type, such as whether the interaction was initiated by the user or the agent/system.

columns.Name: REFERENCE_INFORMATION
columns.Comments: Stores additional reference information, such as a candidate's phone number, for the visitor use case.

columns.Name: REFERENCE_ID
columns.Comments: Stores an additional reference ID, such as a visitor ID, for the visitor use case.

columns.Name: SUB_REFERENCE_ID
columns.Comments: Stores additional sub-reference information, such as an agent conversation id.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.
